mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate decline, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear periods with a t-stat > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day returns in >65% of observations during recovery phases, or the predictive slope flips sign when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.5% forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent single-series effects in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals that higher borrowing costs are successfully suppressing manufacturing activity, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects immediate consumer spending strength, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following retail sales growth, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year evaluation windows.
